sourceMovie.title: News of the World
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto News of the World (2020) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and western blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Slow West
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If News of the World clicked for you, Slow West (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Little Big Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If News of the World clicked for you, Little Big Man (1970) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and western, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after News of the World?
faq.answer: Try Slow West, Little Big Man, Jeremiah Johnson—each shares DNA with News of the World while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is News of the World?
faq.answer: News of the World (2020) is best described as Drama, Western, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is News of the World worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
